title: Add a hard settle deadline after killTree so a bridge call cannot hang forever when kill fails
evidence: failAndKill sets `reason` and calls killTree, but the promise only resolves from the child 'close' event. On win32, killTree runs `spawnSync(taskkillExecutable, ...)` with no `timeout` option and ignores the result; if taskkill hangs the sync call blocks the whole extension event loop indefinitely, and if taskkill fails (nonzero/access denied) there is no fallback `child.kill` and no secondary timer — 'close' may never fire and `call()` never resolves, wedging the tool gate.
impact: A stuck python bridge child on Windows can hang the gated tool call (and the host session) indefinitely instead of degrading to the fail-direction response after timeoutMs.
recommendation: Pass a timeout to the taskkill spawnSync (as terminateWindowsHelperTree in process-tree.ts already does), fall back to child.kill('SIGKILL') on taskkill failure, and add a bounded post-kill deadline that force-finishes with the pending failure reason even if 'close' never arrives.
acceptance_criteria: killTree taskkill invocation has a bounded timeout and a kill fallback
BridgeClient.call resolves within a bounded window after failAndKill even if the child never emits close
